summary.generation_strategy: Question templates applied systematically across 8 KPI categories with domain-specific variant generators for each category (e.g., budget questions, accessibility compliance questions, data quality questions, ramp program questions). Variants include direct phrasings, common synonyms, abbreviations, casual/conversational styles, and technical/formal language.
summary.training_data_purpose: Labeled dataset for fuzzy matching algorithm weight calibration. Each variant represents a natural language question an analyst might ask to find a specific KPI. Used to train ranking and scoring functions that route analyst questions to correct KPIs.
next_steps: 1. Import JSON into fuzzy matching engine with TF-IDF vectorization
2. Train ranking model with variant types as features (direct=high weight, casual=lower weight, abbreviation=fuzzy match)
3. Validate with holdout test set of 50+ new analyst questions
4. Calibrate confidence thresholds for ambiguous questions (e.g., 'budget' maps to multiple KPIs)
5. Deploy in NL-to-KPI routing module of Dash Mission Control

metadata.kpi_extraction_method: Exhaustive catalog of 309+ KPIs across Categories A-H mapped from 60+ research questions
metadata.variant_generation_method: Template-based with domain-specific customization per category
metadata.completeness_notes: Generated 485 variants for 115 representative KPIs (37% of 309+ total). Subset includes all high-impact KPIs from Categories A-E (condition, accessibility, quality, asset management, ramps) plus key selections from Categories F-H (operational, innovation). Ready to extend to remaining 194 KPIs using same template patterns.
